concept: Swim / Move Through Water by One's Own Motion
description: To swim: the child across the pond, the fish upriver — bodies moving through water under their own power. Water's own motion verbs stay distinct: 'selu' (flow) for the water, 'lupho' (float) for resting on it, 'wishe' for working through it.
sound_symbolism: Bright 'wi' is the moving body, 'she' the hush of water parting around it. The word swims.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive with path: 'lopia thue melothe wishe.' (The child swims through the pond.) Of fish: 'shalu mua luphore wishe.' (Fish swim in the stream.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Swimming is effort in right relationship with a medium — push too hard and sink, surrender entirely and drift. The middle way, wet.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Rivers were roads; swimming was the ferryless crossing, taught early where water bordered life.
